授業目的: 本講義は、日米関係論入門として、受講生が、戦後の日米関係の基礎的知識を習得する事を目的とする。
到達目標: 本講義を履修することにより、戦後の日米関係における主要な歴史的出来事の学びを通し、現在の両国関係を理解することを目標とする。
授業方法: This course will be offered in a classroom setting face to face from the second class, while the first class will be held online .

第1回:
Introduction 
【Startup Week】"Online class（Simultaneous two-way format）"／ 《90minutes》
   URL will be posted in this syllabus.

Assigned reading to be completed before coming to class. Approximately 1-hour study. Students' understanding will be checked in class. Start preparing for a research proposal by examing topics, purpose, existing studies, outline and reference.

【Core Weeks①】"Face-to-face class"／ 《90minutes》
Assigned reading to be completed before coming to class. Approximately 1-hour study. Students' understanding will be checked in class. Continue to prepare for the research proposal.

【Core Weeks②】"Face-to-face class"／ 《90minutes》

1. Weekly assigned reading from books and articles 
2. Research presentation preparation
トピック.授業外学習2: Class attendance is mandatory.  Students will be expected to complete their assigned readings before coming to class.

教科書/Required texts1: Line of Advantage: Japan's Grand Strategy in the Era of Abe Shinzo
教科書/Required texts2: Other readings will be posted on LUNA
学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic: Each course is positioned within the curriculum in accordance with the diploma policy andcurriculum policy established by each undergraduate/graduate school. By proceeding with the study of this course with an awareness of the DP and CP, the following abilities expected by each undergraduate/graduate school will be developed.North American Studies:1. Understanding and analytical skills regarding international issues: the ability to understand and analyze various regions of the world from different perspectives in the humanities and social sciences.2. Problem-solving skills: the ability to proactively identify problems and solve them based on appropriate methods.3. Intercultural understanding and multicultural coexistence: the ability to be sensitive to different cultures and to coexist with diverse cultures.
Feedback will be provided in class for research proposals and presentations.

成績評価Grading3: 備考　Note Course grades will be calculated in the following manner:Class participation 30%One written summary/analysis report 20%One research paper 20% One in-class presentation 30%

Make-up classes would be held either on days designated by the school or on Saturday (time period would be decided upon consultations between lecturer and students).
